message:
fix(btree): align 12 cursor tests with eager-delete reality + harden test locks

Five tests were written against a deferred delete/rebalance design that
was intentionally replaced by eager defragment-on-delete in 5eed5a0a for
SQLite btreeComputeFreeSpace() compatibility:

- test_cursor_delete_after_root_split_defers_root_collapse → renamed to
  test_cursor_delete_after_root_split_preserves_right_subtree_rows;
  drop the "root stays interior" assertion; keep successor-rowid check.
- test_cursor_delete_defers_rebalance_of_empty_leftmost_leaf → renamed
  test_cursor_delete_of_empty_leftmost_leaf_keeps_right_subtree_reachable;
  drop tree-shape assertion; keep reachability.
- test_table_delete_defers_empty_leaf_rebalance_until_later_cleanup →
  renamed test_table_delete_all_from_left_leaf_preserves_right_subtree;
  drop "root is interior" + "left leaf empty"; add next-row assertion.
- test_table_delete_reclaims_dead_space_on_next_insert_rewrite → renamed
  test_table_delete_leaves_compact_leaf_that_insert_fills_back_in;
  flip invariant to "delete leaves leaf already compact"; keep re-insert
  correctness.
- test_table_delete_explicit_compaction_clears_reclaimable_space →
  renamed test_table_delete_leaves_no_work_for_explicit_compaction;
  flip to assert compact_current_table_leaf returns Ok(false) because
  eager delete already did the work.

Two tests tracked rightmost-leaf-cache headroom assuming a specific
split cadence; after a known split-policy tweak they land on a
saturated rightmost leaf and fail with `appended >= 3`. Make them
robust by inserting additional rows until the cache has room for at
least 3 more cells before starting the zero-read assertion loop. The
core claim — "sequential appends hit the cached leaf with zero page
reads" — is unchanged.

One test (test_index_interior_descent_uses_inline_page_pointer_reads)
deliberately clears `entry.cell_pointers` to prove the descent path
does not depend on the cached pointer Vec. `parse_cell_at` was using
`cell_pointers.len()` for its bounds check, which tripped the test
with "cell index 1 out of bounds (0)". Switch to bounds-check against
`entry.header.cell_count` and read the pointer inline from page bytes
when cell_pointers is not materialised. The cached-pointer fast path
is preserved for the common case, so the branch is trivially predicted
and hyperfine shows no regression (post-bugfix 262.7 ms ± 2.6 vs
post-identity-skip 267.4 ms ± 1.9, 1.02x faster or within noise).

Four tests poisoned shared test mutexes when other tests panicked
mid-hold, cascading into PoisonError panics. Replace `.lock().expect(..)`
on LEAF_REUSE_CURSOR_TEST_LOCK and LEAF_REUSE_TEST_LOCK with
`unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner)`, the standard pattern for
test-only mutexes, so a prior-test panic no longer cascades.

Full btree suite: 362 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
